name: ChatGPT for Sales
description: OpenAI's conversational AI assistant used by sales professionals for email drafting, prospect research, role-play practice, objection handling preparation, and proposal writing. ChatGPT is the Swiss Army knife of AI tools for sales.
whyUseIt: ChatGPT accelerates almost every writing and research task in sales. Draft a cold email in 30 seconds. Research an industry in 2 minutes. Practise a discovery call by role-playing with the AI as the buyer. Generate objection responses. Summarise a long research report. The reps who learn to use AI effectively will outproduce those who don't by a significant margin.
aiCapabilities: Full conversational AI with internet access for research, document analysis for account intelligence, code interpretation for data analysis, and image generation for presentation visuals.
funnelPosition: Full funnel — productivity acceleration across every sales activity
sellerWorkflow: Before a call: 'Research [company] and summarise their top 3 business challenges.' After a call: 'Turn these notes into a follow-up email.' For outreach: 'Write a cold email to a VP of Sales at an Australian SaaS company about our forecasting platform.' For practice: 'Act as a sceptical CFO and let me practise my pitch.'
managerExpects: AI is used to accelerate work, not replace thinking. Emails should be human-reviewed and personalised before sending. Research should be verified against primary sources.
demoDescription: Draft a personalised cold email, research a target account, role-play a discovery call, generate objection responses, and create a competitive comparison matrix — all through conversation.

vocabulary.definition: The skill of writing effective instructions for AI to get better outputs. In sales: specifying the persona, context, tone, and desired format.
Background instructions that shape the AI's behaviour — like telling it to act as a sales coach or a buyer persona.

beginnerMistakes: Using AI-generated content without editing — buyers can spot generic AI writing instantly

Not providing enough context in prompts — 'write a cold email' produces garbage; 'write a cold email to a VP of Sales at a 200-person SaaS company about our forecasting platform, referencing their recent enterprise hiring push' produces gold
Trusting AI research without verification — always check facts against primary sources
Not using AI for practice and role-play — it's a free, available-24/7 training partner
